name: Monogate
description: Single binary gate (EML: exp(x) − ln(y)) proven to generate every elementary function as a finite tree. EML Weierstrass Theorem proven. Bivariate Weierstrass confirmed empirically (5/5 targets MSE<1e-3, 3/5 near machine-epsilon). Infinite Zeros Barrier confirmed across 1.7B trees. EML-SIREN beats sin-SIREN 4/5 spatial targets.

capabilities.name: EML Universal Dense Approximation
capabilities.description: Linear combinations of EML tree atoms are dense in C[a,b]. All tested functions are DENSE: 0 separations, 0 exceptions across all univariate and bivariate targets.

capabilities.name: Infinite Zeros Barrier
capabilities.description: No single EML tree (without linear combination) can represent sin(x). Proven analytically and confirmed by exhaustive enumeration of all 1,704,034,304 trees through N=12.

capabilities.name: EML Weierstrass Theorem
capabilities.description: EML linear combinations are a universal approximation basis for all continuous functions on compact domains. Direct proof: ln(x) is an exact depth-1 atom, x^n = eml(n·ln(x), 1) is exact at finite depth, so all polynomials are in span(EML) exactly. Classical Weierstrass closes.

capabilities.name: Bivariate EML Weierstrass Theorem
capabilities.description: The span of bivariate EML trees with leaves in {1, x, y} is dense in C([a,b]²). x·y = exp(ln x + ln y) is an exact bivariate EML tree, so all monomials x^m·y^n are exact and bivariate classical Weierstrass closes.

constraints.key_identity: x·y = exp(ln x + ln y) — exact bivariate EML tree

capabilities.name: Jackson-Type Exponential Rate Bound
capabilities.description: For real-analytic f on compact K ⊂ (0,∞): F(N) ≤ C·ρ^N (exponential convergence). For C^k functions: F(N) ≤ C_k·N^(-2k) (polynomial convergence). Proven via Taylor containment in the EML polynomial subalgebra.

capabilities.name: EML-k Complexity Census (23 functions)
capabilities.description: Computable function complexity measure: EML-k(f) = min{N : F(N) < ε₀}. EML-1: {exp, ln, constants}. EML-2: {x, 1/x, √x, rational functions, sinh, cosh}. EML-3: ALL standard transcendentals (sin, cos, tanh, Gaussian, erf, Bessel j0, polynomials x²–x⁵). Universal 10⁴–10¹⁸× phase transition at depth 3.

capabilities.name: Exhaustive Tree Enumeration
capabilities.description: Rust implementation enumerating all EML binary trees to N=12. Processes 1.7 billion trees in 2m46s on 8 threads.

capabilities.name: EML-SIREN Coordinate Networks
capabilities.description: EMLLayer activations replace sin in SIREN coordinate networks. EML-SIREN outperforms sin-SIREN on 4/5 spatial targets (SDFs, Gaussians, inverse-square) with the same parameter count. Best improvement: 49× lower MSE on inverse-square law.

capabilities.name: Symbolic Distillation of Coordinate Networks
capabilities.description: Trained EML-SIREN networks are distillable to compact EML symbolic formulas via MCTS. Achieves 400–1201× parameter compression with interpretable, mathematically exact output formulas.

capabilities.name: Bivariate EML Basis Fitting (Weierstrass Empirical Confirmation)
capabilities.description: Ridge regression over 32 polynomial + 4 radial EML atoms fits all 5 bivariate targets (circle/ellipse SDF, Gaussian, ring SDF, inverse-square) with MSE < 1e-3 in-sample and out-of-sample. Three targets reach machine-epsilon MSE (< 1e-7). Directly instantiates the Bivariate EML Weierstrass Theorem numerically: polynomial features ⊆ span(EML) + radial atoms (exp(−r²), 1/(r²+ε), √r) are all finite-depth EML trees.

benchmarks.name: Bivariate EML Basis: OOS MSE (best target)
benchmarks.notes: 5/5 targets MSE < 1e-3; 3/5 targets near machine-epsilon. Poly degree-6 + 4 radial EML atoms.

proofs.name: Infinite Zeros Barrier
proofs.statement: For any EML tree T of finite size, T(x) − sin(x) has only finitely many zeros on any compact interval. Therefore no EML tree equals sin(x).
proofs.proof_method: Real-analytic Identity Theorem + exhaustive search of all 1,704,034,304 trees through N=12.

proofs.name: EML Weierstrass Theorem
proofs.statement: For any compact K ⊂ (0,∞) and any f ∈ C(K), the closure of finite linear combinations of EML tree atoms equals C(K).
proofs.proof_method: ln(x) exact depth-1 atom → x^n = eml(n·ln(x),1) exact finite depth → P[x] ⊆ span(EML) → classical Weierstrass.

proofs.name: Bivariate EML Weierstrass Theorem
proofs.statement: For K = [a,b]² with 0 < a < b, span of bivariate EML trees with leaves in {1,x,y} is dense in C(K).
proofs.proof_method: x·y = exp(ln x + ln y) exact bivariate EML tree → all monomials x^m·y^n exact → bivariate classical Weierstrass.

proofs.name: EML Jackson-Type Rate Theorem
proofs.statement: For real-analytic f on compact K ⊂ (0,∞): F(N) ≤ C·ρ^N. For f ∈ C^k(K): F(N) ≤ C_k·N^(-2k).
proofs.proof_method: Taylor containment in polynomial subalgebra P[x] ⊆ closure(EML).
